food_subgroup: Vegetarian protein wrap — paneer-based flatbread roll

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Paneer Kathi Roll; closest parent is flatbread wrap food product (FOODON_03315091).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for Paneer Kathi Roll specifically; A0FHV (wheat flour flatbread) is the closest defensible parent category covering the roti/paratha component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Paneer Kathi Roll or sufficiently close parent concept in clinical food terminology.

main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, chilli)

serving.natural_language_portion: One Paneer Kathi Roll typically weighs approximately 200 g, comprising one griddled whole wheat paratha wrapped around a spiced paneer filling with vegetables and chutney.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Paneer and composite wheat-based preparations
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry exists for Paneer Kathi Roll as a composite dish. Values are derived by combining ICMR-NIN data for paneer (per 100 g), whole wheat paratha (per 100 g), and mixed vegetable filling components, weighted to reflect a typical recipe composition: approximately 35% paneer, 40% paratha, 25% vegetable and condiment filling. Energy and macros cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central entries for cottage cheese (FDC ID 173420) and whole wheat flatbread (FDC ID 175036). The resulting per-100 g composite is a best-estimate derivation clearly documented here.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — whole wheat flatbread / chapati analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Paneer Kathi Roll. GI of 52 is estimated using the GI of whole wheat chapati/roti (GI ~52, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care) as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. Paneer contributes protein and fat which further attenuate postprandial glucose response, supporting a low GI classification. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 49.0) / 100 ≈ 25.5, using 200 g serving with 24.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~310 mg per 100 g; ~620 mg per roll) from spice blends, chutney, and paneer processing
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a roll and request reduced salt in preparation, as a full roll can contribute approximately 27% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~25.5 per roll) driven by refined or semi-refined wheat paratha; protein and fat from paneer partially attenuate postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume one roll as a complete meal rather than a side dish, pair it with a non-starchy vegetable side, and choose whole wheat paratha to benefit from the lower GI (~52) and higher fibre content.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and ghee (~4.5 g per 100 g; ~9 g per roll) and moderate sodium load
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to one roll per occasion, request preparation with minimal ghee or substitute with a small amount of unsaturated oil, and monitor overall saturated fat intake against the daily limit of less than 20 g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Paneer Kathi Roll is a popular Indian street food and restaurant dish originating from the Kathi Roll tradition of Kolkata, West Bengal, and now widely served across North India and in Indian restaurants globally, including Singapore. The dish consists of a griddled whole wheat paratha (flatbread) wrapped around a filling of spiced paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) that has been marinated in yogurt and spices such as cumin, coriander, turmeric, garam masala, and chilli, then pan-fried or griddled. The filling typically also includes sliced onions, capsicum, tomato, and a drizzle of green mint-coriander chutney and lemon juice. The paratha is cooked on a tawa (griddle) with a small amount of oil or ghee until golden and slightly crisp, then rolled tightly around the filling. The texture is a contrast of soft, slightly chewy flatbread exterior with a tender, spiced, mildly tangy paneer interior and crunchy raw onion. Nutritionally, Paneer Kathi Roll is a moderate-energy food (~218 kcal per 100 g) providing meaningful protein (~9.2 g per 100 g) from paneer, a good source of calcium (~180 mg per 100 g) and phosphorus. The dish has a low glycemic index (~52) due to the whole wheat base and the protein-fat matrix from paneer, making it a relatively balanced meal option compared to refined-flour alternatives. Sodium content is moderate (~310 mg per 100 g) and should be monitored by individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. It is vegetarian and halal-suitable, making it accessible to a broad dietary population in Singapore's multicultural food landscape.

How many calories in a Paneer Kathi Roll?

confidence_reason: No single ICMR-NIN or USDA entry exists for Paneer Kathi Roll as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from a weighted combination of ICMR-NIN data for paneer and whole wheat paratha, cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central. GI is an analogous estimate based on whole wheat chapati/roti from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than high because the values represent a best-estimate composite rather than a direct laboratory measurement of this specific dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are a weighted composite estimate: approximately 35% paneer, 40% whole wheat paratha, 25% vegetable and condiment filling, derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods and cross-checked with USDA FoodData Central (FDC IDs 173420 and 175036). 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for these component foods. 3. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for these component foods. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are approximate. 5. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on whole wheat chapati/roti (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for Paneer Kathi Roll was found in approved sources. 6. Serving size of 200 g is a typical restaurant portion estimate; actual weights vary by vendor. 7. The food_group 'Meat and alternatives' is retained from the input record as paneer is the primary protein source and functions as a meat alternative in Indian vegetarian cuisine. 8. Halal status is conditional on paneer and other ingredients being sourced from halal-certified suppliers; this is typical but not guaranteed at all vendors.

improvement_actions_taken: Constructed composite nutrient profile from ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central approved sources with documented weighting methodology.
Applied GI analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database (whole wheat chapati/roti GI 52) with full documentation.
Added Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names to satisfy Indian cuisine multilingual requirements.
Documented all -1 sentinel values (vitamin_d_mcg, selenium_mcg) with explicit source-gap justification in notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list for perfect consistency before finalising.
Set gi_category to 'low' consistent with GI 52 ≤ 55 threshold rule.
Used only permitted ontology match_type values: exact, closest_parent, none.
